goal.objective: Complete the V11 Specialist Compiler release candidate through an independently reviewed two-phase prelaunch audit with an exact external verification receipt, a hardened first-run reference host, self-sufficient generated handoff contracts, publicly closed specialist schemas, deterministic artifact media and normalized evidence controls, exact candidate analysis, raw handoff and dependency fan-in verification, canonical gates, traceable integration, and owner merge gate without claiming runtime orchestration.

assumptions.statement: The six work units are stable atomic responsibilities for this release review.
assumptions.rationale: They were derived from the accepted V11 spec and have disjoint review or integration ownership.

assumptions.statement: Every repository context source is byte-identical under the declared Git checkout rules, with no custom filter or working-tree encoding.
assumptions.rationale: A reviewed launch package must reconstruct from a clean checkout, not only the integration owner working tree.

assumptions.statement: The integration owner retains separate approved compilation and package digests for the prelaunch audit and, only after its PASS, for the candidate package.
assumptions.rationale: Each package verification is trustworthy only when its expected digest pair comes from an independent approval record, and candidate launch approval follows the audit gate.

assumptions.statement: The external IDE host enforces actual context, permission, workspace, and process boundaries.
assumptions.rationale: The V11 compiler emits provider-neutral task demand and performs no runtime effects.

assumptions.statement: Any release document that integration may update is reviewed through an immutable pre-integration snapshot rather than its live output path.
assumptions.rationale: Authorized integration must not invalidate the exact candidate it consumes.

assumptions.statement: The external host preserves each specialist result as exact raw UTF-8 bytes before parsing and supplies the complete dependency set for fan-in.
assumptions.rationale: V11 can verify supplied bytes and package identity but does not execute agents or persist runtime evidence.

assumptions.statement: Work, startup, and handoff values are relative planning units rather than elapsed-time forecasts.
assumptions.rationale: V11 compares deterministic candidate structure and does not claim runtime benchmarking.

assumptions.statement: Canonical release processes use only temporary workspaces that resolve inside declared .local/npm-cache/** write authority.
assumptions.rationale: Functional success is not authority-compliant evidence when tests inherit system TEMP/TMP; a verified Windows short-path spelling may reduce path length only when it resolves to the same authorized target.

assumptions.statement: An agent-based semantic review of the exact candidate compilation uses a separate read-only audit package rather than a work unit inside that candidate.
assumptions.rationale: A compilation cannot include its own complete bytes as authenticated context without changing itself.

unresolvedDecisions.question: Which IDE, models, and workspace isolation strategy should execute a future production roster?
unresolvedDecisions.proceedRationale: Runtime supply is explicitly outside the V11 compiler acceptance boundary and requires a later adapter decision.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Candidate construction and analysis, scheduling, selection, handoff identity, dependency closure, and digest semantics are deterministic and accurately claimed.

evidenceRequirements.description: Content-addressed algorithm and test candidate sources.
Independent algorithm and lifecycle acceptance review.

acceptanceCriteria.description: The V11 public compiler, candidate-analysis, package, handoff, fan-in, first-run reference host, IDE, and exported schema APIs match the owner goal and remain IDE, model, provider, runtime, private-path, and network neutral.

evidenceRequirements.description: Content-addressed product and API candidate sources.
Independent product and API acceptance review.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Read-only first-run with bounded closed approval parsing and a current retained approval, exact receipt-bound prelaunch authorization, strict public schema registry, both clean packed-consumer hosts, canonical verification, raw handoff assessment, integration replay, and source-linked release evidence are complete before owner approval.

evidenceRequirements.description: Integrated V11 review, memory, and milestone artifacts.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Authority, context, privacy, strict retained-approval parsing, canonical contained source reads, rendering, raw handoff, deterministic artifact media, normalized controls, proxy and accessor, package, fan-in, and trace boundaries fail closed.

evidenceRequirements.description: Content-addressed security and trace candidate sources.
Independent security and trace acceptance review.

Do not execute or dispatch runtime agents from core.
Do not merge or update durable memory before integration review.
Do not widen reviewed filesystem authority.

stopConditions: Stop with REVISE if a blueprint can widen owner authority.
